title: Adhatoda leptantha
intro: Adhatoda leptantha, also known as Vasa or Adhatoda, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of India and Sri Lanka, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Adhatoda leptantha include alkaloids like vasicoline and vasicinone, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Adhatoda leptantha include decoctions, infusions, and powder formulations, often used in traditional medicine to alleviate respiratory symptoms and boost overall health.
taxonomy: Adhatoda leptantha is a species within the Acanthaceae family of flowering plants. The genus Adhatoda is a group of plants commonly found in tropical regions. Adhatoda leptantha is further classified as a member of the Acanthaceae family, which is part of the Lamiales order. The classification of Adhatoda leptantha reflects its evolutionary relationships to other flowering plants. Its classification also provides information about its growth habits and habitats.
